attacking_style: Launches decisive moves in final 1-6km on summit finishes, often countering or bridging before executing a late surge. Favors attacking at/after climb summits rather than on steep lower slopes (800m-5.8km to go documented).
team_tactics: Frequently operates with minimal team support, executing solo tactical plans. Israel occasionally commits full team resources early for specific objectives (KOM jersey), but Woods often self-sufficient in finale positioning and decision-making.
terrain_preference: Excels on steep puncheur climbs (Mur de Huy, Basque cols) and rolling/hilly finales requiring repeated efforts. Strong on sustained summit finishes but struggles on technical descents and nervous flat approaches where crashes occur repeatedly.
vulnerability: Chronic crash risk on technical descents, wet roads, and flat nervous sections (5 documented crashes 2017-2022). Lacks explosive acceleration after sustained efforts; gets dropped by immediate post-summit attacks. Tactically vulnerable in sprint positioning (corner control, final 100m).
key_patterns: Repeatedly crashes on descents and technical/flat sections, eliminating stage contention (5 incidents documented)
Strong bridging ability to lead groups but vulnerable to immediate accelerations after sustained efforts
Effective late-climb surge 0.8-6km from finish, catching riders or creating gaps in final meters
Tactical sprint intelligence: refuses cooperation, takes psychological angles, but lacks pure finishing speed
Demonstrated GC awareness via bonus second collection over stage hunting when positioned well overall
Descent weakness flagged repeatedly as tactical liability despite climbing strength
form_trajectory: positive: 2023 TDF stage win at 36 years old catching younger riders with exceptional late-climb power suggests career-best timing and sustained form management into late career
built_from: 2022 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 3
